May no assignment policy pa, pero honestly, gumagana ba? Ang pinaka-problema, parang naging normal na yung pagod. Parang expected na laging may [REDACTED_NAME], laging [REDACTED_NAME], laging naghahabol. Walang pahinga na totoo. Kahit weekend, may guilt ka [REDACTED_NAME] ka [REDACTED_NAME] may nakatambak. Hindi naman [REDACTED_NAME] nagrereklamo [REDACTED_NAME] naming mahirapan. Nagsasalita lang [REDACTED_NAME] over na sa sobra. [REDACTED_NAME] hindi [REDACTED_NAME]. Ang edukasyon hindi karera. Hindi rin dapat [REDACTED_NAME]. KUNG PWEDE LANG HATIIN KATAWAN NAMING GRADE 12 STUDENTS, GINAWA NA NAMIN. This semester is sooo draining in every way possible. I don’t care anymore about the open forums created by admins of the offices because [REDACTED_NAME]. Are you even hearing us out? You tell us to rant and report properly (e.g., open forums) and WE DID. Last semester you gathered class reps from all sections to share our concerns as students. Every one of us participated, spoke out, and was a voice, crying for help, and hoping for visible changes. Yet, everything is still the same. PeTa here, WW there, assessments everywhere! We’re not even given enough time for our [REDACTED_NAME] anymore [REDACTED_NAME] “naghahabol” nga sa school calendar. [REDACTED_NAME] katatapos pa lang ng PeTa sa ganitong subject, MAYROON NA NAMAN! [REDACTED_NAME] pasahan at quizzes. Hello??? [REDACTED_NAME]??? Paulit-ulit na lang po ba??? Everyone’s acting like they actually care when in fact [REDACTED_NAME]. Isama niyo pa ibang teachers [REDACTED_NAME] stress instead of helping us. We’re already struggling to keep up, ganyan pa? If the shoe fits, wear it. Give us time for ourselves as well. All our weekends are being sacrificed na; our family time, our reset, our sleep, our health, and our time to do some leisure activities outside school. We’ve been begging and asking for help and change multiple times now; nakakapagod maghabol [REDACTED_NAME] namang planong magbago. If my words are harsh, just imagine how much harsher the reality is; the demands and workload we’re facing as students.

to the freshie guy from [REDACTED_NAME] (i won’t mention your program but you know who you are, with the glasses) [REDACTED_NAME] soy and sesame, happy holidays! i’ve been meaning to say this for a while but honestly, i didn’t have the courage to say it to you directly. this isn’t a “kiss and tell” or anything like that, i just... i wanted to let you know, in case somehow you see this. i’m not really the type to go to bars or clubs, but there was this one time that i decided to go with my roomies. we ended up having a really good time. then, the second time i went out, we went to soy, and that’s when i met you. we were having fun, just laughing and talking, and i remember thinking, wow, you’re genuinely kind and such a nice person. i was happy to have met you, even if we weren’t super close. the third time i went out, i actually went there because of you. i got drunk, and the people around us offered us drinks tapos you took care of me without even realizing it. you made sure i was safe, you stayed with me, and even booked a grab for us without making me pay. i honestly appreciated that more than you’ll ever know. and then... well, when we got to your place, we did something i never expected. it was my first time with a guy, and i had a lot of confusing feelings about it at first. i blamed myself initially, but the truth is, i liked it. i liked you. it wasn’t just the moment, it was you. you’ve stayed in my mind ever since. i think it’s because from the start, you were already my type. and even though that night happened, nothing about our dynamic changed—you were still the kind, respectful, and genuinely nice person i met that night. you told me i was your first too, and that meant a lot. honestly, i just want you to know that i fell for you. i think about you more than i probably should, not because of what happened that night, but because of who you are. you’re attractive, yes, but more than that, you’re kind, thoughtful, and somehow just make me feel safe and cared for even without trying. i don’t necessarily miss that night specifically, but i can’t stop wanting you. i can’t stop thinking about you. you’re just... stuck in my mind in the best way possible. i hope this doesn’t make things awkward. i just needed to say it, somewhere, somehow. so, hi. i hope you see this. i hope you know that you’re genuinely a great guy and that someone out there really, really admires you. i fell for you, and honestly, i don’t regret it at all. happy new year :))

lemme rant hi, to those students under Ms. [REDACTED_NAME] — you’ll understand what I mean. Since the start of the semester, I’ve been piling up my feelings toward how she handles her classes. During online sessions, she easily gets mad when students don’t respond fast enough, even if the reason is something simple like a mic or camera issue. It’s difficult to ask questions or raise concerns because she’s always so strict, both in class and in Teams. now I just gathered the courage to share this, especially after reading how many students have also spoken up about their experiences with her. I realized I wasn’t alone in feeling this way. yes, I understand she wants discipline, but sometimes her strictness feels too much. It’s draining — we already have so much to deal with, and it feels like she doesn’t even try to understand our situation. We get tired too, and you’re not the only subject we manage. We’re students juggling multiple tasks and responsibilities, and sometimes all we want is for you to listen and understand where we’re coming from. Not all students are in the same phase of intelligence or can do things as fast as you expect. We all learn and process things differently. I can take criticism, but yours is too much. There’s a fine line between correcting and embarrassing someone, and that line was crossed when you called me out in front of the class last month. That moment stuck with me — it hurt, and it made me lose the confidence to even attend your class. It wasn’t that I didn’t care; I was just tired, hurt, and overwhelmed. A little empathy goes a long way :)

[REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] and [REDACTED_NAME] were there, but we only stayed up to [REDACTED_NAME], cuz we were so thirsty, hungry and sore. I saw the newspaper by the journalist being sat on, stepped on, and littered around the campus, there are SOOO many places to throw your trash in, why on the ground? Pero bakit [REDACTED_NAME] opening, e kung [REDACTED_NAME] ust samin na kung INDIO kami??? When we were roaming around the UST campus and we keep getting these glares from the students like we did something to offend them, it really felt like we were unwelcomed. The UAAP opening and the concert was held was at a field, the field was wet so [REDACTED_NAME] shoes, my shoes looked like i stepped in one of Willy Wonka's chocolate contraptions. ALSO, when we went to eat at uncle [REDACTED_NAME]'s after the concert, and i overheard a girl dressed in her pajamas she said "nangangawit [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME], bat pa kasi may mga tiga ibang school pa?" Alam mo, tama ka, [REDACTED_NAME] ng bagay o problema ay dapat ilahad kung [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME] mabuhay at hindi umunawa. There are some hardships that are meant to be kept no matter how much you want to let it out. The term "endure" or tiisin" is a negative term for them because you want to feel your struggles as a blessing as not all of the struggles can be treated with gratitude; some must surpass desperately to move on and forget about it. If you're enduring your program you're not enjoying [REDACTED_NAME], endure it; if your environment is toxic, endure it; your failures and bad days? Endure it. Tiisin mo lng at keep it to yourself. Think of the freedom you'll have once you've endured all of that. I am writing this after my father na nangnermon about my feelings, about my rants, about what I'm currently through that he couldn't understand. I know why people who are in my age range are keeping their problems to themselves, they know no one would listen, they know they would receive humiliation than comfort. I am also writing this to tell myself that everything is going to be fine, you'll get out from the toxic environment you're in, and you'll be living in the dream you've always dreamed about. Pero may isang ipapakiusap [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] if someone approaches you para ilabas kung anuman nais nilang ilabas mula sa kanilang saloobin; if you're with the people you know who would drain you but couldn't get out of the same room because you have to, tiisin mo na lng and learn from them, learn how to deal and navigate people like them; if no one wants to help you, help yourself because even the people around us are unable to help themselves, [REDACTED_NAME], feel proud because you didn't let yourself down unlike them. You only have yourself and yourself is your best friend but remain an open mind to those who are in need. Invest and take care of yourself. You're in this world for a reason and I know life will be hard but you'll make it.

Earlier today, an unfortunate incident occurred within our school that I believe deserves thoughtful attention and reflection. A number of my co-students were denied enrollment due to their inability to pay the full amount of tuition fees upfront. What is troubling is that these students have consistently demonstrated financial responsibility in the past and have settled their obligations diligently, albeit sometimes through delayed payments. Despite this, no alternative arrangement, such as a promissory note, was offered or considered. As many are aware, such measures are intended to support students facing temporary financial hardship, allowing them to continue their education while fulfilling their obligations responsibly. Moreover, the situation was handled in a manner that raised concern. A member of the administrative staff addressed the issue by raising their voice, which many found unnecessary and disheartening. For students already struggling, this approach can be demoralizing and may create a learning environment that feels hostile rather than supportive. This is a respectful call for greater sensitivity and understanding, especially toward students who remain committed to their education despite financial limitations. Educational institutions should be safe spaces where compassion and fairness are consistently upheld.

I’m not trying to be mean or ungrateful because I know the organizers and the family council put in a lot of effort to make the graduation ball happen. I truly recognize and appreciate the time and energy it took to pull it off. But to be honest, I still can’t help but feel that the ₱5,300 I paid wasn’t fully justified. I understand that a big chunk of the budget likely went to renting the ballroom at Solaire, and yes, it’s a prestigious venue—but that choice came with a cost. It’s just funny to me how many students ended up asking the same thing: “Bakit ang mahal?” and “Bakit walang guest artist?” When in the first place, the decision to book such an expensive location was made knowing the limitations it might put on other aspects of the event. Then the theme. The theme was supposed to be a masquerade, which should’ve brought a sense of mystery and anonymity. But in the end, it didn’t really feel like that concept was achieved. It ended up feeling like just another formal gathering with masks on the side, rather than a true masquerade experience. I’m saying this not to discredit anyone’s hard work, but to express a valid concern—not just mine, but one that a lot of us seem to share. I hope that moving forward, big decisions like this are made with more consideration for both the vision of the event and the students who are investing in it.

As a student who genuinely cares about where our tuition fees go, I can no longer stay silent about the glaring underperformance of several student organizations at [NCR] High School. These orgs are funded by our money, and yet many fail to serve their purpose or meet even the minimum expectations of student engagement, leadership, or innovation. Take BYTES—both the President and Vice President resigned due to academic load. While academics matter, let’s not forget that they voluntarily ran for these positions. Leadership is not about convenience. It’s about commitment, especially when you're entrusted with public responsibility. Resigning midway only reflects poor foresight and misplaced priorities. TAMCAST has not produced any meaningful multimedia content. For a group tasked with showcasing stories and school culture, their silence is disappointing. Where’s the creativity? Where’s the output? Its silence and inactivity are a disservice to the talents within the community and to the purpose it supposedly serves. KAPS, meanwhile, feels more like CEES’ errand team than an independent academic org. What initiatives have they actually pioneered? SPARTAMS claims to champion eSports and athletics, yet even a simple, well-organized tournament remains unseen. Their SAILS activity lacked quality, direction, and impact. How can an esports org not even manage to run a proper gaming event? Then there’s ESO, JPO, and AYEA. Honestly—what's their relevance? With the presence of STEM Society, GoCare, and ABM Society, those three have become nothing more than copy-paste versions of existing organizations/office. Instead of duplicating efforts, why not redirect those resources to performing orgs that actually bring life and value to the campus through passion, talent, and output? Student organizations are not for show. They are meant to build leadership, engage the community, and enrich student life. If one cannot commit, then one should not apply. The student body deserves better usage of both representation and resources

For those na mag-thethesis palang jan, I would like to give ya'll a quick tip that I should've followed myself... Firstly, DO NOT group up with your friend group, you may think you already know your friend group from the inside and outside, trust me you don't and being in a thesis group with them will reveal toxic traits about them that you were never able to see before if you keep looking at it subjectively instead of logically. Second of all, make sure the topic ya'll wanna do from the very first time ya'll passed the title defense is something ya'll wanna do, and something ya'll are somewhat knowledgeable at so that nobody will feel out of place simply because the topic ya'll are doing is outside of someone's area of expertise. Another one is do not be overly too ambitious specially if you know your group will struggle to deliver the project on time on the long run. Third, if you are the project manager make sure to take the job seriously and avoid groupmates who does not listen to you and keeps deciding things for themselves. I am saying all these because it's the things I should've followed because from where I am standing right now, I regret every decisions I have made for our thesis starting with grouping up with my friend group. As a project manager, my groupmates never listened to me from the very beginning that could've helped make things easier for us and now I am leading a project that is way outside of my expertise and I feel useless and out of place that it made me ask, am I actually in the right group? No matter how good someone is at project management, if the project is simply too ambitious, way out of your area of expertise and your groupmates do not listen to you, magaganahan ka pa ba maging project leader and lead this project till the end?

It’s disappointing to see the FEUHS COMELEC Vice Chairperson treat the issue of abstained candidates during the elections as a laughing matter — even going so far as to react with “haha” reacts on posts shared by strandmates. As members of the COMELEC, there is a responsibility to remain non-partisan and professional at all times. Elections are meant to be fair, serious, and credible — not something to be made light of, especially by those tasked to ensure the integrity of the process. Given that the election system itself is still VERY FAR from perfection, the focus should be on improving procedures and rebuilding trust, not on displaying unprofessionalism online. When the system isn’t solid yet, the least that can be expected is for its leaders to model neutrality, respect, and seriousness. Positions of trust demand better. Moving forward, I hope the COMELEC leadership takes these concerns seriously and commits to a higher standard of conduct — not just for the sake of appearances, but to protect the legitimacy of the elections and the confidence of the student body. You have a fair share on why the student government elections had this results.

Dear Ico, I don’t even know if I’m still in love with you. But every time you cross my mind, and you still do, more often than I’d like to admit, I find myself wishing it was me. Me who you chose to spend your life with, your love, your days, instead of my friend. I keep wondering, if I hadn’t mentioned her to you, would you have ever looked at me differently? Would you have seen me, really seen me, and maybe felt something more? Maybe it’s the hopeless romantic in me, but I did things for you without expecting anything in return. I was always there, quietly, unconditionally, even when I knew it would break my heart. And I never asked for anything, because your happiness meant more to me than my own feelings. I want you to know that I’m happy for the both of you. I truly am. But that doesn’t change the fact that deep down, I still catch myself thinking what if? What if it was me instead of her? What if there was a version of us that actually had a chance? I know it’s all just wishful thinking now, but sometimes those thoughts feel more real than the silence between us. Sincerely, chi

Disclaimer: This post is to raise concern and educate based on my experience. It’s not intended to shame or attack the professor involved. I understand that I can’t control what happens when this is shared or how others comment. The photo isn’t mine, so I’ve covered it, and the information mentioned was from the professor's own discretion—not official course material. WHY IS MY PROF LIKE THIS? He always talks about professionalism and high standards, which I actually respect. There’s nothing wrong with aiming for excellence and being strict with deadlines or performance. But here's what I find unfair: He said only the first 3–5 students who pass can possibly get a perfect score. The highest raw grade is supposedly just 86 for those who pass not late but still on time. Before he does by day which is good! [REDACTED_NAME] it incentivizes those who pass it ASAP along with everyone else but now its per person?! Mind you, this is in a comlab setting (na mabagal) where we all answer at the same time, and in the only section for Data Science, which includes all our scholars and students who also strive for high grades. So you're telling me that: Even if a bunch of us do well, we still can’t all get 4.0 and pano yung ibang scholar [REDACTED_NAME] mapababa? Our performance is limited by quota and not by effort or output? Some people say he’ll “curve” the grades, but honestly, I find this disheartening. This isn’t incentivizing for hardworking students— It’s punishing for everyone. We’re told to aim high, but the system makes it feel impossible by design, so gusto nyo professional setting so he does this real-world bullshit in class, in real world we can call unto DOLE or HR easily. Is it really too much to ask for a grading system that actually rewards competence? - edited [REDACTED_NAME] so yall cant find me by conyo ass typings

The Constellation of Almost : The summer I turned nineteen, Manila felt like a fever dream—loud, restless, and alive. Jeepneys choked the streets, neon signs blinked without rhythm, and the air smelled like a mix of rain, smoke, and street food. España Boulevard pulsed like a heartbeat, crowded with students, sidewalk vendors, and the late-night hum of tapsihan. [REDACTED_NAME]’s eatery still stood then, its flickering lights calling out to anyone who needed a warm meal and a place to belong. I was both homesick and hungry. I had just graduated from college—back when it was a quick sprint, not the drawn-out hustle it is now. I was a small-town boy with big dreams, thinking the city would be easy to figure out. It wasn’t. That’s when I met him. [REDACTED_NAME]—from [NCR]’s Marketing Department, a volleyball player. He had braces, wore confidence like it was borrowed, and stood near [REDACTED_NAME] like he was waiting to be noticed. He wasn’t conventionally charming, but he had the kind of presence that made you curious. When our eyes met again, he asked if I wanted to go home with him—sabay ka sa akin pauwi, he said, casual like it wasn’t the first time we’d spoken. Not come with me like a promise, not stay like a plea—just a simple offer from one stranger to another, as if sharing a commute could mean something more. Looking back, I realize naivety is just another form of belief. You don’t know you have it until you find yourself in a stranger’s room, heart racing, pretending you’re not scared. Outside his window, the city sprawled endlessly—Cubao, Tandang Sora, names I barely knew. He told me which taxis to avoid (never trust the white ones, he said), and I memorized it like a secret. His room smelled like a mix of mint and mildew, and when he kissed me, I pretended not to notice that his hands were shaking. The next morning came, plain and uneventful. He mixed coffee into his rice—something I’d never seen before—and I laughed. Maybe because it was absurd, or maybe because laughter was the only thing that kept the silence from stinging. His walls were filled with graduation photos of his siblings, some proudly holding UP diplomas. I’ll call you, he said, scribbling his number on a paper. But the universe isn’t romantic. The calls never came. [REDACTED_NAME]—or maybe it was Facebook—grew silent. I learned how a single night can stretch into years of wondering. One moment, one boy, can become a compass you quietly use to navigate every city street and failed connection that comes after. : Even now, I still remember the details: the jeep ride from España to Cubao, the green and yellow taxi lights, the song playing on his radio—Snow Patrol’s “Chasing Cars.” I’ve replayed it more times than I’d like to admit. [REDACTED_NAME], I wonder if you ever think of me—the boy who mistook a spark for a sign, who spent years turning silence into stories. You were my first supernova—bright, unforgettable, and gone too quickly. And even though I wished for a second chance, I’ve come to accept the truth: we weren’t a love story. We were just the beginning. A short chapter written in taxi fares, static-filled songs, and coffee-stained memories. So here’s to the almosts—to people like [REDACTED_NAME], who disappear like Manila sunsets: sudden, breathtaking, and impossible to hold. I no longer wait for signs or stars to align. But sometimes, when the sky is clear and the night is quiet, I still look up—just for a moment. (And if you’re reading this—don’t worry. I drive my own car now.)

One thing I really dislike about group work is dealing with freeloaders. It's frustrating having to carry the load while others barely do anything. Some members don’t even reply or put in less effort than the bare minimum. Being the leader doesn’t mean we have to do everything. Back in the first semester, I gave it my all because I was so motivated to graduate with honors. But this semester? Group projects are seriously draining me. It’s gotten to the point na where I feel so discouraged that sometimes I don’t even feel like going to class anymore. Just showing up feels like a struggle. When group members aren’t responsive, it leads to late submissions, and instead of working as a team, it ends up being just a duo or trio doing all the work—which makes everything even harder. What’s worse is that everyone still gets a grade, even if the contributions were clearly unequal. Sure, there’s peer evaluation, but it still doesn’t feel fair when the ones doing the work are left exhausted and overwhelmed. You guys know what I’m aiming for—and you’re supposed to be my friends. So instead of helping out, why are you just watching me struggle? [REDACTED_NAME] maging responsive?

So disappointed to see the rise of AI creating art right now, especially seeing it here in [REDACTED_NAME]. Just a second ago I saw [NCR] Lady [NCR]' post wishing their team a good luck on uaap. Only instead of the irl picture of the team, they decided to hop on that tiktok trend of using the ai ghibli artstyle filter. [REDACTED_NAME] is literally one of the many creative people that are PUBLICLY AGAINST AI! Another time I witnessed ai being used here was back in OPC. Itchyworms was performing and I loved their performance, the energy, and the line up. However, throughout their set list I noticed that their accompanying visuals on stage's screens had some animations that were suspiciously ai generated. I didn't think of it much at first until they did their second song and another visual in a different artstyle but same ai animation was played! then again with the third song until the last. (Just check out [NCR] advocate's short reel of itchyworms and tell me the art in the back isnt ai. I have a video of another ai animation they used but [REDACTED_NAME] HAHAHAH). I was also shocked on how NOBODY called them out on this. For me, I had the same level of resentment for this as when [REDACTED_NAME] chose to have an ai-generated video to win their animated music video contest over hundreds of artists that spent time and effort on making their genuine animations. The rise of using someone else's art to feed into an ai machine then using it to generate the most generic shit is tiring at this point. And most of the people who do use ai (other than those who claim to be "ai artists that are as good as hand artists") don't understand the underlying problems that this leaves us with. Over and over again there has been waves of information against ai slop but people choose to ignore it. Artists whose medium is not traditional art also disappoint me. PWouldn't they be enraged if someone used their voice to generate a song and then claim to have the same talent and rights to being paid for music as the singer? Isn't it absolutely vile when someone takes someone's face and deepfakes it onto p0rn? Doesn't it make your blood boil when a piece of literature generated by ai gets praised over writing made with genuine emotion and time? This isn't a "ban ai for all" statement, because I do believe that ai can help ASSIST us in many ways. I just can't help but be angry that ai is continuosly being used to steal someone else's work or in cheaters' output that is worse than if they tried to create something themselves. Use it to study, use it to organize your files, use it to outline your ideas, use it to make your animating life easier, but don't generate your whole work on it then say "I worked hard for this". It should make the process easier, not do the whole thing for you. The computer is over-heated and you're not even breaking a sweat. HOLD YOUR ARTISTS ACCOUNTABLE.

It is with deep concern and disappointment that we address the glaring deficiencies in the performance of the [NCR] High School Commission on Elections (COMELEC). As the primary body tasked with upholding democratic integrity and ensuring the smooth facilitation of student electoral processes, the COMELEC Executive Board has failed and significantly did not meet even the most basic standards of competence, organization, and responsibility. Despite the gravity of their mandate, the current COMELEC has demonstrated a consistent pattern of disorganization, lack of transparency, and administrative ineptitude. Timelines have not been adhered to, clear communication has been absent, and procedural guidelines, if they exist at all, have either been poorly implemented or completely ignored. These lapses not only undermine the credibility of the electoral process but also erode the student body’s trust in the institutions that are supposed to represent their voices. Worse still, the student body is now left bearing the consequences of COMELEC’s negligence—having elected a new set of student government leaders who, to this day, remain unacknowledged and unsupported by the very commission that oversaw their election. To be entrusted with such a critical role, and yet show such an alarming level of unpreparedness and irresponsibility, is both unacceptable and disheartening. This is not merely a failure of execution. It is a failure of leadership, of integrity, and of service. If the COMELEC cannot uphold the duties it was created to perform, then a serious reevaluation of its current composition, structure, and leadership is not only warranted it is imperative.

to Hello! FEUHS - SHS student here. Mahihirapan ka especially sa una if hindi mo kayang mag handle ng overwhelming activities, but overtime masasanay ka rin and mama-manage mo naman. Environment is fine, just be with the people na you know will be good for you. Profs are very okay (atleast for me). Regarding [REDACTED_NAME] workloads and schedules, as I said, overwhelming pero you have to learn to manage; sa schedule okay naman, maa-adjust [REDACTED_NAME] clock mo talaga. Sa uniform, type a is only worn during homeroom schedules (Monday if batch a and Tuesday if batch b). Type b naman is worn during Tuesdays and Mondays or simply kapag hindi niyo homeroom schedules and wala kayong PE sched, PE is only worn [REDACTED_NAME] may PE kayo or if [REDACTED_NAME] niyo sa homeroom days. Additionally, type C also exists which is any [NCR] shirt that is worn with any pants/skirt that adheres to the dress code. [REDACTED_NAME] this helps and answer your questions!

I’ve been incredibly lonely for years, especially since the pandemic hit. I've lost touch with friends and, as a homeschooled person, the isolation has only grown. Every day feels like I’m stuck in my own world, just trying to find something to hold onto. So I'm gonna tell you what I'm like. I need someone to talk to. I’m reaching out because I’m desperate for real human connection. It’s hard to keep going like this, and I don’t know how much longer I can be in this silence. I love cats and animals. they’ve always brought me some comfort, and [REDACTED_NAME] has is my favorite hero. After all, I've always been into the cuteness of things. Music is a huge part of me. I play guitar, piano, ukulele, bass, and more. I dream of starting a band one day. I love to watch anime and analyze animes. Vocaloids are another passion of mine, shoutout to all the [REDACTED_NAME] fans. I’m into gaming, especially FPS games and Minecraft, but sometimes it feels hollow when I’m playing by myself. I also like [REDACTED_NAME]! for a bit of challenge. I also draw, edit videos, and write stories. and because I do, it brings out the "funny" out of me. I just wish someone could hear it out. I speak fluent English and some 日本語. I spend time thinking about philosophy and science, wondering about life and the universe, but it feels like all these thoughts are trapped in my head with no one to share them with. I feel like I’ve been trapped in my own world for too long, and it’s getting harder to deal with the silence. I don’t want to keep feeling this way. If there’s anyone out there who understands what it’s like to feel alone for so long, please reach out. I don’t care who you are or what you love—I just want to talk to someone. I’ll listen, I’ll be there. I just don’t want to keep feeling invisible. discord tag: fertilesquirrel711

It’s my first time doing this and I really wanted to get this off my chest for the past few weeks. I told myself that I will solely focus on my studies and nothing more than that but I find myself looking at my classmate differently—more than just a classmate it seems. I have a habit of spacing out and letting myself get lost in my own thoughts and even though it wasn’t my intention, I find myself stealing glances of her from time to time. It was as if my eyes have the minds of their own and consciously searching for anything that resembles her. I might end up writing a whole ass novel here but please bear with me on this one hahaha. Continuing on, it got to the point where these simple glance thievery of hers made me question if I feel something sentimental, romantic, or anything that resembles those falling in love kinda trope. Her smile is ridiculously contagious, I oftentimes find myself smiling like a complete idiot. In my point of view she’s that captivating—making you feel like you’re on top of the world and the academic challenges you face on a daily basis feel like they’re just mere inconvenience. I have been asking myself if I certainly am at a point where my feelings for her are set in stone and take the risk, a huge leap of faith in asking her out or is it just nothing but a mere infatuation or romanticizing the idea of being with her. Well thank you for coming to my TEDtalk now go do your damn acads.

Grade 11 student here. I swear, I’m so tired. Like, I know we’re supposed to just deal with low grades, the insane amount of schoolwork, and the constant stress because, you know, that’s life, but I can’t help but feel so overwhelmed. No matter how much I try to push through, it just feels like I’m constantly drowning in deadlines, quizzes, and expectations. And honestly? [REDACTED_NAME] has drained the life out of me. I don’t even know if transferring was the right decision anymore. I thought maybe I could handle it. Like, sure, new school, new system, new everything. It was going to be tough, but I figured I’d adjust. But now? I wake up already tired, spend the whole day running on low energy, and then go to sleep still stressed about what’s due tomorrow. There’s barely any time to breathe, let alone actually rest. And the worst part? No matter how much effort I put in, it never feels like enough. The grades? Yeah, they’re not looking great. And I know people will say, "Grades don’t define you," or "It’s okay, as long as you’re learning," but let’s be real, when you’re in a school that constantly expects excellence and puro events pa, seeing low scores just feels like a slap in the face. It’s like, great, another reminder that I’m not doing well enough. And yeah, I get that failure is part of the process, but when you’re already exhausted, failure just feels like confirmation that maybe you’re not cut out for this at all. And the workload?? Insane. Like, I get that this is supposed to be preparing us for college, but at this rate, I won’t even make it to college without completely burning out. I miss the days when school didn’t feel like this. When I didn’t have to worry about whether I’d survive the next grading period. When I actually had time to enjoy things instead of feeling guilty for resting. I’m certain that I’ll never have the courage to step up and tell you this face to face but this goes out to the person I met somewhere around september . I genuinely pray you’d never walk pass by me along the hallways of [NCR] with anger and hatred in your heart thinking that I cut you off. I stayed even when it ended, I stayed even after all the confusion and tears you caused me. It hurts to think that all I ever did was to be there for you and it felt like I was just forcing myself to fit in your world again, I wasn’t even the reason why you got tired let alone pushed me away, it was because of you. If you keep on insisting that I never liked you or say that you never noticed, I hope you find humor in that as much as I do. God knows how willing and obvious I was to go out of my way to make you feel loved and noticed especially at times when it feels like it’s hard to be you, when you feel like you’re a failure because the crowd doesn’t turn their heads to see you. Would I have gone all the way to españa to get you a gift for your birthday knowing damn well there’d be a typhoon and I’d get sick the day after? Would I have gotten you your favorite drink knowing that you gave up so much sleep just to excel in a quiz even at the expense of your well-being? Would I have gone all the way back to school just to support you on your presentation because everyone were too busy to stop by your stall? I prayed that you’d be the one but given your situation, I knew that praying for you to find your drive in life was a lot more important than what I had been praying for because you needed me more as a friend than a partner, and while finding closure over the fact that sometimes two people can’t beat the odds was something I was able to take into heart, I don’t think I could ever process the courage it took you to send all those texts to your bestfriend about how they should have never said anything in the first place because they created a “second opinion“, how you’re not ready yet you find someone and then suddenly you’re doing everything we did with her, how it was so easy for you to say “bakit? [REDACTED_NAME] ba araw-araw nag uusap?“. May point [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] ikaw [REDACTED_NAME]? It really didn’t matter to me if you couldn't be my whole world as long as I’d have the whole world to experience with you but after what happened, how could I ever live and grapple with the weight of traumas, pain, and confusion you’ve bestowed in my world? I genuinely hope that next time you meet someone again, you learn how to love them not out of habit, but out of choice. Not as an obligation, but as a privilege. Not as an afterthought, but as a priority. I still have so much within me, so much questions about why you had to put me through all that unnecessary pain but you know what? I’d rather keep some things left unsaid, because somehow, I find peace in not knowing and letting go of all the curiosities that could potentially open wounds. God Bless, future tech student :)

i would like to have a short sentiment on this and other related fws Maybe SOME students do change their study habits and improve their time management, yet despite all their efforts, they still receive low scores—scores that do not always reflect the hard work they put in. Perhaps the issue is no longer with the students but with the way teachers formulate exam questions. Often, these questions are overly complex or unclear, making it difficult to demonstrate true understanding. In the end, grades still play a significant role in shaping one's future, whether fair or not. To that certain issue, I hope these people get what they deserve for putting all at risk. Also, to a certain person, each person faces unique circumstances, challenges, and responsibilities. It is neither fair nor appropriate to impose one’s experiences as a standard for others, nor is it our duty to justify or align our lives with someone else’s expectations.

hi bestie, if this showed up on your feed then this reading is for you ❤ no hashtags and no subject mentioned, [REDACTED_NAME] take what resonates and leave what doesn't 🥰 Anyways, kidding aside pero I honestly think some teachers should start showing more consideration for their students. It already takes a long time for grades to be released, yet there seems to be little consideration for students. How are we supposed to keep track of our academic performance when we only find out our scores once grades are about to be submitted? By then, there’s nothing we can do to improve them anymore. I understand that we can personally ask our teachers about our scores, but if that’s the case, what’s the purpose of using Canvas if our grades aren’t reflected there? How are we supposed to use the "What If" scores to strategize on improving our performance if our grades are locked? How can we identify and correct our mistakes if assessments aren’t even returned to us? More importantly, how can we truly learn if we don’t know what we got wrong in the first place? I’ve been an honor student my whole life, and I’ve never been 'pabaya' when it comes to academics. My friends and peers are the same. That’s why it’s incredibly frustrating to see how our grades are turning out this time. For the first time, we’re receiving really low grades, which is something we’ve never experienced before. I don’t know if [REDACTED_NAME] may ganitong issue, but it’s honestly really difficult to accept. [REDACTED_NAME] at this point, I’m starting to believe that at least half of the student population might be struggling with low scores as well. If we check the mean scores of assessments, that should be enough to support our case. If the majority of students are struggling, then shouldn’t the teachers also reflect on this? It’s not just about students not studying hard enough or lacking focus—there’s clearly a bigger issue at play. If more than half of us have low grades, then the teaching methods or assessment strategies should also be questioned. This isn’t solely the students’ fault anymore.

Why are some people unable to take responsibility for their academic tasks? In my GED class, we were assigned an activity that we could only complete in pairs or groups of up to three members. Since I didn't have a partner almost two weeks before the deadline, I reached out to one of my classmates to see if she had a partner. She replied asking if I wanted to pair up with her. I was uncertain, but I was too shy to say no, so I agreed. We quickly decided on an internal deadline and divided the work. Since the activity was a critique, I suggested that we wait for each other's parts before starting to ensure the ideas were integrated. We picked our parts, with the agreement that I would do mine after she completed hers. However, it’s been almost two weeks now, and she still hasn’t finished her part. Tomorrow is the midterm exam, and we had agreed to finish everything before then. She confidently agreed to this, but now, I’ve been asking for updates, and I haven't heard anything from her. She mentioned earlier that she was busy with her major subjects, but honestly, don’t we all have the same workload? I’ve been trying to understand her situation, and I even agreed to extend the deadline, but at this point, I’m frustrated.

tl;dr: Freshie at [NCR] doesn't like his batchmates' ghosting-habits and overreliance on AI. (pic unrelated, its just funny) Freshman at [NCR] here, and I loathe every second I'm in a class, face-to-face or online. I need to get this off my chest because I've been ranting about this to about everyone I know on campus for the whole two sems I've been here, and I'm still so pressed about it that I genuinely am deciding if it's really worth it to stay here. No, I'm not going to talk about the school itself, nor its faculty, nor its general staff, nor the SCC, nor the orgs. They have their own problems, but it's not like every college doesn't have its own issues. No, I'm going to talk about my fellow classmates and batchmates; the people I have to rely on in my groupworks and the people I have to be with for 4 whole years. Let me tell you my experiences here. First semester wasn't actually all that bad. Our section was small, and so we knew each other pretty well. However, when midterms rolled around ? I was swamped in it. I could handle a stressful situation, but to this extent ? I needed a helping hand. This specific midterms project was a simple presentation proposal, so how hard would it be ? Apparently, very hard ! Out of the 5 total people in that group, 2 would actually sit down and work on it. I would have thought that to be the end of it but no, apparently not, because I still had to be stuck with them for finals too ! When finals came around I could not handle the inactivity anymore and dropped out of the group to transfer to a new one, essentially dissolving the group to do their own thing. Nobody bar one-two people would chat in the gc, and only when it was a week until the deadline of our finals output did people actually start moving. I understand that there are people that work or have their own families to take care of, more power to you. But you have to keep us in the know ! You can say that you had an important call or something happened that you need to attend to all you want, but you also have a duty as a student to talk to your group if something happens, otherwise it's like you're ghosting us just to come back a week before an important deadline to ask what we're going to do ! You're not the only one that has a grade in this, dinadamay [REDACTED_NAME] lahat. By second sem, I had the idea to have different sections for my classes. It would be fun, I said. It'd fit my schedule better, I said. I'd meet the rest of my batchmates, I said. While all true, that also just gave way to more chances of things previously said happening again. And boy, have I had that experience more than I can count. Without missing a single one, every group work, every group midterms, every finals output, its the same thing. We make the group chat, we sit on our asses until the deadline rolls around and we PANIC ! I try to say something in the gc but [REDACTED_NAME], zip, nothing ! Its like I'm cursed to have at least one person that's essentially dead weight until a timer dings and it SUCKS ! You want to know what's worse than having shitty groupmates ? Shitty groupmates that use AI. Regardless of your views on the use of Artificial Intelligence, I think it's safe to say that using it heavily in your work- you know, your thesis, your papers, your outputs, your quizzes, your exams, all that- feels like you're just using school as an excuse to go out. You're not learning anything. You're paying your tuition for nothing. That piece of paper that will be your diploma will mean nothing when you get it. It will be a glorified paper weight by the time you get a job. Your reliance on AI now can never substitute putting actual work into your studies. But sure, if you really just want to graduate for the diploma, go right ahead. [REDACTED_NAME] damayin boss.

Should I be the one to break up with my girlfriend? These past few months, I have been experiencing issues with my mental state. I just know, because it's here again (the thoughts). It has been so hard for me to keep myself sane lately, and it affects my mood towards everyone—*everyone*. I try not to be too harsh, but it's just so much easier to burst everything out. I hate that I can't even get my head straight sometimes and realize how bad my actions were after reacting. I’m so frustrated with myself; I hate this because I’m back to being a hot-headed person. I thought I fixed this years ago, yet here we are again. Both of us are graduating—I'm from IAS, and she's from IABF. We’ve been together for over two years, and I was not like this at the beginning of the relationship, not even in the second year. However, this started about four months ago. I began to get more heated. It feels like the longer we’ve been together, the worse it gets. I am trying. I really am. I try to keep myself in check, but regardless of my efforts, the anger just comes out eventually. Going back to my first statement, I’ve been thinking about breaking up with my girlfriend. I always wonder what will happen if I do. Will we be able to get back together once I feel better? I don’t want to break up with her. We’ve already discussed our plans for the future, and I am so excited to welcome that future with her. If it weren’t for this, I wouldn’t even consider breaking up. I’m scared to be alone while I work on myself, and I want her by my side. I love her, God knows how much, but I feel like she might need this break more than me. One thing that stops me from doing it is because I want her to focus more on her studies. Both of us are graduating soon, and I don’t want her to stress about this, especially with graduation just a few months away. Also, I sometimes feel like she wants to end the relationship, but also feels like she’s not ready for that moment. I feel like she loves me so much that she doesn’t want to let go of me. I think the love she has for me stops her from doing that. After a few months away, I feel like she might want to break up with me. That’s why I was wondering if it would be better for us to break up, or should I try and figure things out for myself while still being in a relationship so I don’t affect everybody? Or should I just sacrifice our relationship to save her from me? Please I need help a more rational approach to my problem.

To the Administration of [NCR] Alabang Senior High School, I am writing to express the growing concerns of students regarding the overwhelming workload and financial strain that the current academic system is placing on us. As student leaders and learners, we are expected to balance heavy responsibilities, but the demands have reached a point where it is becoming nearly impossible to manage both effectively. One major issue is the unrealistic nature of certain project requirements. For instance, being tasked with creating a full Roblox game simulation for a minor subject is excessive, especially when many students do not have access to laptops or PCs capable of handling game development. This requirement assumes that all students have the resources to complete such a task, which is not the case. The financial burden is also a significant concern—I have already spent over ₱3,000 within a single week solely for project expenses. This is unsustainable, and many students are struggling to keep up without additional financial support. Furthermore, the sheer volume of deadlines leaves no room for rest or proper learning. The workload is structured in a way that prioritizes task completion over actual understanding and student well-being. We understand the need for academic rigor, but the current approach is unrealistic, exhausting, and inconsiderate of students’ financial and mental health. Additionally, there is growing concern that some faculty members lack consideration for students’ well-being. Education should be a two-way process where both teachers and students work together to foster learning, but there is a clear disconnect when teachers prioritize their requirements over students’ capacity to meet them. We urge the administration to reevaluate the workload, financial demands, and overall system in place. Students should not have to choose between their well-being and their education. We request fairer academic expectations, more financially accessible project requirements, and more empathy from faculty members when it comes to deadlines and workload.

Hello po sa admin, please allow our survey po. Thank you [REDACTED_NAME]! Greetings Fellow [NCR]! We are Grade 12 STEM students of [NCR] [REDACTED_NAME]. As a partial fulfillment of our III and Thesis Writing subject, we are collecting data for our study titled "Exploring the Link Between AI Application Usage and Cognitive Functions: Insights of Pre-Medical Students from Selected Universities and Colleges in Luzon". The purpose of this survey is to recognize which AI applications students frequently use and the duration of their usage. By then, the study will be able to examine the correlation between AI application usage and the cognitive functions, particularly the critical thinking and problem-solving skills of the students. Is it my responsibility to reply to every message immediately? Sadly, no. I’m opening this up because a friend of mine from [NCR] tech opened up to me (I have her permission) that her friend is avoiding her or being nonchalant towards her just because she didn’t reply to her friend’s message immediately. This is so bothersome because there was a time when she called me crying, asking me what she did wrong, as if she did something wrong? Which I think she didn’t. Everyone must understand that there are circumstances why we can’t reply to every message immediately. Maybe we are busy, maybe we are in class, maybe we are sick, or perhaps we just don’t have the energy to reply. Everyone must understand this, and please don’t be mad if we can’t reply immediately. We can reply later, or we can talk about your message personally when we see each other. In addition, not everyone is chronically online, so don’t expect an immediate reply. Please, if you know you’re like this, please control your behavior and learn to communicate. Not everything can be resolved with sulking (IT’S DRAINING, I SWEAR). Please, you’re not in high school anymore to act like that. Please be patient and respect each other’s boundaries too. Thank you.

It saddens me that for the first time of my life, this happened to me. I was at the Recto Station and I saw a line of people waiting for those on the platform to board the train first before clearing the way for the next passengers. I tapped my beep card and I saw a senior with her pwd card that they need to go up first. So then I did the same since I also identified as a person with disability with my pwd beep card (i have a hearing impairment). I said to the guard that "may i also go first?", presenting my pwd beep card. She said that If i will go to cubao station but then I said my actual destined station. She then agreed that I will go first, I passed through the line of people. However, suddenly, a kuya guard stopped me. I purposefully showed my pwd beep card, then I said my destined station. He then said that I should not go since seniors are only allowed to go first. I couldn't defend my side since there are people looking at me. I then just said, "okay" to not make a scene na lang and i got back in line. It was embarrassing enough for me and it was my first time that the first thing i did is just agree na lang to what happened. [REDACTED_NAME] looked very defensive, so that's why i decided to just say okay to the situation. I’m sure he glanced my PWD beep card, yet he still denied me the priority access I was entitled to. At that moment, I couldn't help but think, does my PWD beep card even matter if this is how they treat me? Do I not look disabled enough for them to acknowledge my right? Some might say, "Why didn't you defend yourself and show your official PWD ID?". But i was caught off guard. I acted and spoke without thinking since I couldn't stand the way people were staring at me. From their perspective, it probably looked like I was arguing with [REDACTED_NAME], even though it was only for a moment. Kuya guard looks defensive and i didnt know what to say or do. I just accepted the situation. After that, I reached to my destined station and file a complaint about what happened to me. They said I shouldn't complain here, but to Recto station counter because that's where the problem happened. And I just followed to their suggestion that i should write a complaint paper and fill up my personal info so they could update me on any actions taken. After a few days, I still didn't get an update. I'm not sure what will happen na lang.

Good evening, OPC! 1st Year IHSN - Nursing student here. I'd like to vent out and read your thoughts lang with what I have been thinking lately. Before entering college, I was so sure that I wanted to become a medical practitioner—though at that time, I still can't figure out where I exactly wanted to be in the medical workforce. After graduating from SHS, I rushed myself to make a decision for the sake of being 'stable' with my college path. I also struggle physically, emotionally, and financially, which kind of stopped me from making a decision for a while. I started college with a heavy heart. Every time that I attend my classes, I feel like there is always something missing—the love that I am supposed to feel to what I am doing. The moment that I figured that out, the motivation and spark that I am holding on to continue starts to fade. Now, I'm already on my second semester. The thought of shifting to another program/school became stronger. This time, I can't really find the motivation to move forward, or even make effort to do the things that I once wanted—which is scary at the same time. Let me ask you, what should I do? Should I continue pursuing this program despite not feeling the spark anymore, or, take my time to figure things out, whether I'll shift or not? I really loved the path that I chose at first, but suddenly, with the things that stops me from being happy throughout this journey, I am slowly, and worse enough, continously losing its spark. I would appreciate each of your perspectives. Thank you : )

Engineering × nursing couple ain’t for the weak. I’m an engineering student dating a nursing student (both a busy field), and we’re both academic achievers. In the first few months of our relationship, we only got to hang out when we were free from schoolwork and workloads. We mostly communicated through chats and calls, which led to many arguments and misunderstandings before. It's really hard to maintain the rs especially when your love language is quality time and physical touch. After a year, we’ve come to understand that making time for each other is just as important as excelling in our fields. At first, we thought understanding each other’s workload was enough, but we soon learned that relationships require effort. So, instead of waiting for free days, we made the most of what we had—quick calls, studying together, and small gestures of support. There are still days of exhaustion and misunderstandings, but we’ve learned to communicate better and remind ourselves that no matter how busy we get, we should never be too busy for each other. Love isn’t about the amount of time spent together but the effort made to stay connected despite everything.

THE RIDLEY SA CONCIERTO PLS PLS PLS

Save your money and consider attending a better school. Not only is the tuition already expensive, but it is also set to increase soon. On top of that, you will be bombarded with extra fees from organizations, events that you are forced to pay for even if you do not attend, and lab fees that only add to the already high tuition. The quality of instructors is hit or miss, with some being good and others not so much. You may find yourself focusing on minor subjects instead of your major, as they demand more attention. It is also concerning that you may be required to take irrelevant subjects, such as IT courses in a BMA program. Some teachers lack passion for teaching or simply do not show up for class. Additionally, as FEUA is a relatively new campus, it suffers from a lack of resources. There have been instances where classes were left without instructors, yet the school plans to add more courses. The modules used are outdated and recycled from [NCR] Tech, and you might as well just self study on YouTube since it is more beneficial. The student portal is often slow or does not load at all, making it difficult to check your grades. Imagine needing to access important information and not being able to because the portal is down. Pray that you do not become an irregular student here, as the school does not have a proper system in place to accommodate you. Overall, FEUA is a lost cause. It seems that the school only cares about its reputation and the money it earns. I hope that if you plan to enroll here, you have a better experience than I did. [REDACTED_NAME], wag na.

Signs Are you also the type of person to ask these? “If magco-color green siya bukas, edi siya na ang the one,” “Universe, ipagtagpo [REDACTED_NAME],” “If [REDACTED_NAME], it means iniisip niya ako,” “Lord, [REDACTED_NAME], bigyan mo ako ng sign.” We ask for signs just for reassurance that we’re on the right path, hoping that the universe will answer us in ways we can understand. But maybe the signs have been around all along, in the things we’ve overlooked or the feelings we’ve ignored. Like when the one we like just leaves us on read, or when we check their reposts and it’s full of things that don’t involve us at all. Maybe those were the signs we were long asking for, the ones we ignore because they only mean that what we’re asking for isn’t for us. We hope for them to like us, but maybe the signs are showing us that we’re meant to move on, not wait for something that isn’t coming. We wish for clarity, but maybe we don’t want to face it when the answer is right in front of us. Maybe the signs aren’t supposed to guide us to what we want, but to what we need. - Basta si ano

To that guy from IABF, I hope it was worth it. I hope it soothed your ego to hit me up only when it suited you. I bet it gave you that sick satisfaction toying with my feelings when I had been nothing but trusting as you picked apart my walls with poking needles until I simply just opened the gates for you. As pathetic as it sounds, you had me. I liked you, I wanted to be with you, and I couldn’t wait to see you again because you drowned me with I miss you’s every chance you could get and I am no better woman—I miss you just the same. There, too, (I think?) three more words God forbid I admit on this app, to salvage some shame. You had me in the way I’d refused every other guy that came before you. Was I too naive? And were you just a better liar? It makes me nauseous. I wonder all the time, did you really have to dangle your affection like a fucking carrot on a string, knowing you’d leave me starving, wanting. When the time came and you’d run your course, you used me up and left. “When the horse is gone, donkey must do.” Was that all I ever was to you? You’re so unfair. I’ve always known I was better than that. Maybe the fault lies in the fact that I believed you were a far bigger person—one who would never make me feel this small. I have too many words to say that I can’t even begin to write them. All I know is that I don’t wish you the best. I don’t wish to cross paths with you ever again, fuck the stars when they align. I pray you carry this hurt in your chest tenfold as I have. And lastly, if ever you do find true love in another woman’s face, may it remind you of everyone you trampled on to get there.

I would like to express my concern regarding the challenges many students face during the enrollment process. The lack of clear and accessible instructions often leaves students confused and stressed. It would be greatly beneficial if all necessary information were consolidated and readily available on the student central. It’s also disheartening to observe that student councils and organizations are taking on responsibilities that should be handled by official school channels. Technical issues also with the student central and the unavailability of office staff, especially during crucial periods, exacerbate the situation. Calls go unanswered, and work-from-home setups hinder timely responses to discrepancies and technical difficulties. The school should improve its processes to ensure a smoother experience for students in the future. Clear communication, better technical support, and accessible administrative assistance.

To a KENDALL from [NCR]. /PLEASE, INTERACT WITH THIS POST FOR HER TO SEE IT./ On days like this... My eyes are twitching, my heart is pounding unusually, and something is boggling on my mind. Once again, my urge is enabled, my urge to have someone, perhaps, to love someone. Although, it is for a short-period of time, I want to feel that urge over-and-over again. On days like this, I am seldomly busy— where finding genuine care, romantic love, and eternal attention occurs. I admit that, it is off and unfair, with the reason of 'I just wanna love someone when I have time, when I am vulnerable, and thirsty of attention.' But, would I blame myself for being this way? I know that everything is not always easy-going and not always with the good things, thus, love is never convenient. But, I just want to love someone that is convenient. I just want to give love and to be someone who receives love; I just want to be happy. In which temporary love makes its entrance— serving me its purpose, serving the qualities that I seek. As time goes by, along with its convenience, this kind of love makes me tired, drained, and unhappy. Leaving me lost, uncertain, and confused. When will I find the essence of settlement? When will I love the functionality of inconvenience? When will I find my permanent source of happiness? Simply, this is how I am feeling for the past few years I am living. But, this time, on a day like this, I found the essence I'm looking for? I learned through the right person— You? It sounds cliché and stupid to get attached with someone whom I just met in a platform where strangers connect, ChatKool. An hour or two is a bit short for someone to be in-love and to be sure. But, if it hits, it hits you eh. (I got dc from the site. I just copied my user, as we both agree to transfer to a different platform) I'd risk it all, if I could gamble fate. Defying its odds, for one chance, just with you. I'm not expecting anything, but, can you reassure me that our fate is intertwined? or if our stars are not aligned? I'm lost in-depths if you are looking for me too, I became a regular in that site, just to find you. There might be no strings that was mutually attached, but my veins are tied to the thought of you, the thought of us. I'm still finding you, through the ways you told me I should do in CK. An Open Letter to [REDACTED_NAME] I thought it was just infatuation. But it wasn't—it was love. We became close, and for a while, everything felt like it was falling into place. Then you told me about your crush, and I tried to help you, even though it hurt. It didn't work out for you, and somehow, I thought it would be my turn. I confessed my feelings to you, and then I waited—a month, to be exact. When you finally responded, I believed we were okay. We talked, just like before. At the start of vacation, it felt like things might still be normal between us, even if you didn't feel the same. But when we finally saw each other face-to-face, everything changed. I was excited, nervous even, to see you. But then I felt it—you were avoiding me. At first, I tried to brush it off. Maybe I was overthinking. Maybe it would pass. Then came my birthday. You didn't wish me. You didn't come. That hurt more than I wanted to admit. You sent a message saying that you would give me something and take me out sometime. I held on to that promise. But now, here I am still waiting even after a month. My friends tell me I’m being a fool. They’re disappointed in me for tolerating the way you’ve treated me. Maybe they’re right. But I can’t help it—you were the only one who made me feel like I was truly seen. You treated me right at first, only for it all to fall apart after I confessed. Now, I wonder if you always were like this or did being honest ruin us? Were you just pretending to care all along? I'm still reflecting, trying to put myself back together. Meanwhile, you are doing great, moving forward like you didn't break me. Yet, some part of me still hopes and wishes you would turn around again. But I know better now. You're just like the rest of them. —[REDACTED_NAME].

Why is being a Student Leader in FEUA so hard? I'm a SHS student and graduating, and the hardest thing I've ever experienced here has GOT to be being a Student Leader. It honestly feels like I'm working a job that I don't get paid to do, but is required to do. I have to be on call for 7 am to 5 pm, be on call even on the weekends and even during supposed breaks or vacations, I still have to maintain my availability and do tasks as requested. Getting projects and budgets approved is also very hard, it is my first time to have to make do with a small budget for food or for anything really, my first time to have to cut corners so much that even the organizers won't be able to feed themselves. My first time to work so hard, and do so many things that I considered to be selfless, and be told that we should not just think about ourselves, but also othets. We are literally being overworked and somehow all of our endeavors is just for something to benefit us? It is so absurd.

Hello to you, boy! I write not to burden you with the weight of my emotions, but to unshackle myself from the haunting shadow of a confession I have no intention of delivering. My admiration has lived in the unspoken spaces, in the moments where silence screamed louder than words. I crafted dreams in which my feelings were not just acknowledged but shared—a tapestry woven from threads of what-ifs and impossibilities. Yet, like a flower cursed to bloom in darkness, it has withered without ever reaching the sun. You see, I have grown weary of baring my soul only to see it met with indifference or, worse, pity. Love, they say, is brave—but what they fail to mention is how exhausting it becomes to battle with oneself, to wrestle with the fear of rejection, to arm oneself with courage only to find that the war was fought in vain. So, I shall confess no more. No longer will I draft unsent letters or rehearse words that will never escape my lips. No longer will I wait for the perfect moment that exists only in the fantasies of a foolish heart. I will not hand over my vulnerability like a sacrificial offering, only to see it cast aside like an unwanted gift. I choose to walk away, not because you are unworthy of my affection, but because I am worthy of peace. The weight of unspoken love will no longer anchor me to a place where hope feels more like chains than wings. May this Christmas break help me heal from the confessional curse you caused me. Farewell, not to you, but to the torment of longing. I leave it here, in these words, where it can no longer follow me.

To some Members of FEUCSO I have noticed that most of you are having difficulties striking between your responsibilities as a student and commitments to the organization. I believe that being part of an organization is a great opportunity for growth and leadership, but it poses a problem when those functions start to compromise your capability to contribute academically. Leadership describes responsibility and accountability. Duties or roles assumed should be accorded proper care that they be fulfilled both on the side of studies and organizational duties. Coming late to meetings or not contributing to group work due to prior commitments puts a disproportionate strain on your teammates and could make a difference in being able to get things done in time. leadership has nothing to do with titles or recognition. But to get important things done, while the going gets tough, the tough get going. Let's reflect on improving our time management skills as we hold fast to the trust that comes with the role we assume. Remember, Leadership is not something you will acquire for the sake of credentials, leadership is what you acquire for you to be able to perform efficiently towards people.

It's been a long time, but I still find myself thinking of you. Every time we cross paths in that elevator, there’s a bittersweet feeling. It’s been a while, yet my heart still longs for you. I knew from the start that you weren’t ready for us, but I kept pushing myself to be close to you. I kept telling myself I was over it, but deep down, I know I’m not. When I talk about you with my classmates, I can’t help but smile, but inside, it still hurts. I’ve tried to move on, to accept that you don’t feel the same way, but it feels like I keep relapsing. We no longer interact, no longer even say hi, and I can’t help but wonder about the feelings left unspoken between us. But what's most important is that we're both doing okay, just not together. I wish that, at least, we could reconnect as friends, but deep down, I know that this is the last year, and we will become strangers to each other. Just know that I miss you more than words can express. It hurts to see you, happy and fighting, yet so distant. I hope you’re not being too hard on yourself. See you, in that elevator where we always cross paths, where our gazes briefly meet, yet never quite linger. It’s a place where our minds speak volumes, even when our eyes refuse to acknowledge what’s still there. The urge to connect, to say something, lingers in the silence between us, but we keep our distance, trapped in that small space, as if the walls can contain everything unspoken. It hurts, but I’ve come to accept it. We are two people, still existing in the same world, but no longer sharing the same direction. The elevator, once a transient moment of connection, has become a symbol of what could have been, of what was left behind. And so, I let it go. Though the memory of those brief moments still haunts me, I know that sometimes, moving on is the only way to find peace.

I just wanna say that I'm hurt, not too much that it makes me cry or makes me want to scream. But the degree that makes me simply upset. Today, me and my peers, we found out our grades. I was a bit shocked but knew, I deserved the grades that I got, even though some were a bit low. But what I couldn't handle at all was the fact that some of my peers got lower grades, lower grades than what I expected them to get, lower grades than mine! I'm happy that me and my peers passed. We're really grateful, but some of the grades they got were just so unbelievable and something that I didn't expect them to get; we always do group studies and help each other out, even pass papers via online and real life same times and dates; yet their efforts are somehow graded as low? I'm only a student and I don't know everything about what school does, but please, just please, be mindful of each students' effort! Is anyone else getting tired of teachers letting AI do all the work for them? We spend hours researching, writing, and pouring ourselves into assignments, only to have them run through a machine that can’t fully grasp context or effort. It’s like our work is being reduced to a series of procedures, while teachers sit back and let AI dictate what’s "good" or "bad." I've noticed that there’s this growing trend where teachers rely too much on AI detectors (yk who you are, we know who you are.), like Turnitin, to grade papers. These tools are fine for spotting obvious plagiarism, but when it comes to grading content organization, writing style, and especially authenticity? That’s where the line should be drawn. Turnitin can tell you if something’s been said before, but it can’t always tell you if the student said it in a meaningful or original way. Teachers are professionals trained to evaluate and understand nuance, context, and creativity, things AI simply can’t measure. Authenticity isn’t about how unique a sentence looks to any sequences, it’s about the thoughts and ideas behind it. Yes, AI can be precise, but precision isn’t always accuracy. We need to ask ourselves if it’s fair for students to be judged by machines when there’s no real evidence these tools can always get it right. And let’s not forget, writing isn’t a mechanical process. A student might repeat common phrases because some ideas are universal, especially in research. AI detectors can’t always tell the difference between deliberate creativity and standard language. That’s why it’s so unfair to grade based purely on an AI’s results. The human element, understanding intention, context, and expression, can’t be replaced. Teachers, you're in the classroom for a reason. You’re the ones who can see beyond the surface, who understand the nuances of language and ideas. Use these tools if you must, but don’t let them do your job. You’re not just there to monitor students, you’re there to guide, evaluate, and inspire them. At the end of the day, students deserve to be graded by a teacher, not a machine. Sorry for ranting, but our group is really devastated by the grade we received. It’s the same story for other sections too. More than 5 groups told me that during their writing process, they worked together, put in all their effort, and were confident in their work. But when it came to grading, they got marked down due to AI detection in Turnitin. Their teacher didn’t even bother to have a consultation. They said their teacher told them the paper was fine, just like ours did, but in the end, we both got hit with low grades because of the same issue. We were never informed that our work had been detected, so we were comfortable thinking everything was okay, not knowing we should have made revisions. If the AI detector was a factor, then consultations should’ve been part of the process to help us correct the problem. Instead, we were blindsided with no chance to improve. Teachers, please do your work, read our papers, and provide proper feedback. We are students, not machines, and we deserve more than just a grade from an ai.

I loved a man for 2 years. So much that I ignored his red flags. He was the type of person that would not put effort in the relationship, and would just see how things go. I was his very first girlfriend, so I expected the inexperience. He was the type of person that doesn’t do actions on his own, and that if I want anything to happen, I have to tell him. He can never plan dates, never says I love you first, never does anything for me, and I still loved him. I was also sure he loved me, after-all, he did things to me he would never do with anyone else like telling stories about him. He was a conservative type of person, never shared his feelings with anyone. He preferred to be alone, and was surprised I got interested. He was fun to be around. We clicked when it comes to joy and happy times, but never when it comes to sad and angry times. He hated fighting. He can’t stand the thought of it, and was afraid that fighting would end the relationship. He didn’t want to grow. He was too afraid. The relationship became toxic. The same cycle of if something bothered me about him, he’d get upset that I was bothered. He didn’t want to be wrong. He didn’t want to be the bad one, so I kept my feelings to myself, until it got too much. He didn’t want to change for the betterment of the relationship, and its what bothered me. Why must a man keep his pride and ego higher than his love? Why must he rather end the relationship than fix it? We talked about getting married, living our future together, for nothing. It hurts, but I’ll get better. Thank you for loving me Thank you for dealing with me. I’ll treasure the experience you gave me.

**What are your thoughts?** Earlier, I saw a group of students at the women’s CR, and I’m assuming they were freshies. They were a group of friends, and with them were two males who also went inside. From what I could tell, they didn’t do anything inappropriate, just stood around and took some mirror pictures while waiting for their friends. Personally, I feel this isn’t respectful of boundaries. We have gender-neutral comfort rooms for a reason, and entering the women’s comfort room just to wait for your friends or take pictures isn’t appropriate. It made me uncomfortable, and I think it did the same for the other women and the girls with me, as I noticed some looks being exchanged. I get that the two guys are gay, but the fact remains that they are still male. We have Muslim women or conservative women who may not feel comfortable with this, and their privacy should be respected as well. There’s a space for everyone, and i believe it’s important we keep that in mind.
