To you [REDACTED_NAME], my safe place from [NCR], I still think about you, I'm still holding onto you dearly even though you were never truly mine. I still tear a little whenever I remember how you said we will figure things out together. The thought of our rare similarities still gives me goosebumps. I still reminisce that realest three weeks of my life. People might find it odd or overly sentimental to get so attached to someone in such a brief time, but I really do. That was an unforgettable semester break of me—all of the nights we spent, all the knowledge we shared, thoughts we discussed, and life experiences we relate. Our hopes, wishes, corny jokes, and cries transformed and opened some parts of me, shifting how I see and live my life forever. Perhaps, this attachment stems from the deep longing within me—a yearning for a soul with whom I can truly connect, a sanctuary where my truest self finds refuge. A companion who mirrors my own journey, where even the smallest shared moments weave a rare and profound bond. I still dream of you tasting my sinigang, attending concerts of CoJ and [REDACTED_NAME] and sing tenerife sea will all our hearts. I still imagine us stargazing with radwimps songs in background as we lose ourselves in the night. I still look forward on experiencing our favorite season together, to sing you more R&B songs, to lose ourselves in thrillers and horrors, and to figure things out on our own terms. Maybe one day, in some random street, whether in busy corridors, or a quiet grocery aisle, we will cross paths again. We may either rediscover the depth of our connection or come to understand that we were never quite as fitting or ideal as we once thought. I don't know how long it will take to move on from you, I guess it will always linger as a cherished memory. But if time will permit, I hope you’ll still want to journey through Greece with me. To P, the Nursing Student of [NCR], I don’t know if these words will ever find their way to you, but I need to let them out, even if they’re just whispers in the wind. We were happily talking, and the next thing I knew, you were gone. Just like that. No explanation, no goodbye—only a cold, empty silence where your voice used to be. I’ve searched for you, tried to find our conversation, only to discover it’s gone, as if we never even existed in each other’s lives. Did I do something wrong? Was it something I said or the way I looked when you finally saw me? I’ve replayed this so many times in my mind, trying to figure out where it all went wrong. It feels like you erased me, like I wasn’t worth a simple explanation. And that’s what hurts the most. You were the first person in a long time who made me feel seen, who made me believe that maybe, just maybe, I was worth something more. I thought we had something real, something worth holding on to. But now, all I’m left with are questions that haunt me in the quiet moments, when I wonder what it was that made you leave so suddenly. If you’re reading this, I just wish you would give me an explanation as to why you did that, because I don’t know, I have no idea why you just suddenly disappeared. But even with all this pain, I can’t bring myself to hate you. I still wish you the best, P. I hope you find whatever it is you’re looking for, and that you’re happy, wherever you are. If this is how you needed to move on, then I’ll have to accept it, even if it breaks my heart. Take care of yourself, P. I’ll carry the memories of what we shared, brief as they were, and hope that one day, I’ll understand why you left. Until then, I’ll keep searching for closure in the silence you left behind.

We bonded over the same games and over the fact that we both got hurt. I never thought I would love you, but here I am missing you. Before I fell in love with you, we're best friends and I don't want to ruin that because you have been one of the best people I've met. I wish I could say that this is just a phase something that'll eventually stop something that'll go away, but as weeks or maybe months go by these feelings continue to grow and grow without me knowing. I didn't know at first but when you moved away to go and study, it made me realise that "Oh shit, I love him" the distance between us two made it impossible for us to hang out. With the distance separating us, I couldn't ask you to hang out, to go to the arcade, or even coffee dates where we'd always talk about the games that we both played. It took me a while but now I know for a fact that I love you. With this new but old feeling I have for you it made me scared, because in the long run I know it'll start being obvious to our friends, that I'll start acting different and they'd notice. I'm scared because I know once you find out things will never be the same between us, I'd rather hurt myself with these feelings I have for you than lose our friendship.

I just want to share this story of mine. It was 6 in the evening and only now the first drops of rain began to come down. It was raining outside when I was walking towards my sister’s building all in an attempt to avoid getting wet. Having started to walk, I noticed several guys in front of me who were talking and having a good time. I did not pay much attention to it, carried on walking, increasing my speed. And then out of nowhere, one of them got up and walked up which to me and stood next to me wrinkling his face and smiling. “Hello there, do you mind if I borrow your umbrella? Unfortunately, I left mine, but it's just near here." This took me by a surprise but I noticed that he really meant what he was saying. I just smiled and allowed him to accompany me, We made small conversations as we strolled in the rain. Rain made everything worked so real, so quite. When we got to his stop, the friends were there, joking around, telling us that we looked like boyfriend and girlfriend. I could not help but get shy a little and saying goodbye and quickly left walking through the rain feeling quite light. The boy was wearing a black T-shirt.

On a random saturday, even as I'm restless, even with no wink of sleep, my thoughts wander back to her. The chatter and endless bickering of people around me won't suffice. It won't drown her out—she was like a ship. She'd soar the barbarous waves of the massive ocean inside me, just to stay afloat—constantly rattling the myriads of thoughts that swam beneath her. In the wake of my seldom tranquility, the waves serene, she drops her anchor in the deepest depths of my waters. Means not even physical occupancy can sink her, what more the defening silence? The undisturbed sky? The modest blow of the wind? My thoughts would tiptoe to her, my eyes glued to the sky. I found myself yearning for a sign. A sign across the stars, to fuel or to cease—I do not care, but I know what I hope for. As if heartbreak was my birthright, as if every inconsequential event, every step, every tick of seconds—even the very moment I level my head— was calculated by fate. The moment my eyes landed on her . A fervent, almost lightning ache welled up inside me. For a moment, I was stunned. I couldn't move. The serene ocean inside me froze, every other thought with it. Every other thought but her ship, it stayed afloat like it ought to. The world stopped. For once it stopped for me, maybe it found her as beautiful as I did as well. My eyes won't tear away, it won't. She was beautiful, she was deserving of every synonym ever, even pulchritudinous was an understatement. Every waking hour of yearning for her brought me to where I stood frozen, my heart went rigid. It wasn't like a traffic light, because I wasn't warned of the sudden halt, the sudden flash of red. I wish I was, although I don't know what I'd do if I was. But like I saw green, I was defridefibrillted back to life as she looked around when her friend pointed in my direction. I ran. Away. I wanted to hide, I'd take a pillar, I'd take a pole—anything. I was thinking of all the ways to hide from them—from her. Every other noise were muffled, I looked back at where she was. She was looking at where I stood before, her eyes squinting, arms crossed, lips puckered slightly in a pout as she struggles to find what—who her friend was pointing at. As she struggles to find me. She can't, won't. Won't know that she quaked my world in her random saturday hangout. Won't know I'm still trembling even as my world steadies. But I found her. As the ocean inside me thaws back to its waves, another thought swam beneath the frozen surface. Would she have given me a sign if she could have seen me? To my dearest ray of sun, will you send a sign across the stars?

I just want to be loved. Summer of August. For the whole summer, I felt like all that weighed in my mind was the want and need to be loved by someone. I keep talking to ghosts, images, and the wind about how I want to kiss someone, hug them, care for them, be family - have a family, to finally stop saying, "I'm going to die alone." I've always thought about love. I loathe love...but seeing people have it easy makes me sick to my stomach. "Why can't I have that?" That's what my mind hears every time. Not just in summer, but also during stormy nights, heavy days, happy ones, and even during self-love...I'm sick of self-love. This summer was the hardest one, as far as I can remember. Went out to have fun, got there and got hit by how lonely I am - I'm just running away from it. I don't mind couples, I love 'Love', but I admit I do get jealous. I want to feel like a girl too...and a little bit of me dies every time I realize that I could be but just choose not to because I'm so used to being independent, alone, having to be a strong character because I don't have a choice. I have lots of love to give, but I keep giving it to the wrong people. I want to care, but remembering this makes me want to throw up and wish the ground would just eat me. People make mistakes, yeah, but I don't know how many more I could take. Giving my love and care away to people who don't do the same, I like to blame myself for putting up with shit and not learning from it. This much patience doesn't help, it never does and never will. This summer really became a moment of realization of the same things over and over again. I know it's internal issues, but I can't help but think - "If I had someone to lean on, would this be different?" Maybe this is just be being the hopeless romantic I am. Or maybe I just haven't faced my issues properly. But really, I just want to be loved. I want to know how it feels. Just for once, I want something to happen the way I want. . . .

This is the first time I’m confessing to someone, so I’ll keep it anonymous. If I don’t express this feeling, I might just go crazy. So, if you happen to be reading this and the events sound familiar, maybe this is meant for you. I was waiting rather impatiently for my friends at Lerma. Minutes turned into hours, and hours into more hours. I honestly lost track of time, just watching for them to finish their class. Then suddenly, a familiar guy appeared! I know this guy all too well—I’ve had a crush on him for a while now, so I got flustered (though only in my mind). Our eyes met twice, and I had no idea how to react. I tried so hard to avoid meeting his gaze a third time because I knew I’d crack a smile—the smile I was desperately trying to hide. He went to the store in front of me and bought an RC or a Coke—I can’t remember. He turned to face me, and I tried so hard not to look, thinking, what if he was looking at me? Or maybe it was just my imagination. He then chugged his drink, and I laughed internally because, seriously, how do you chug that without gassing up? You know what’s crazy? I started liking him the moment I saw him laugh and smile—it absolutely melted me inside. He radiated so much positive energy, and I couldn’t help but smile too. Anyway, he left after that, and I found myself looking for him, though I tried to be subtle about it. If the universe decides that this boy is meant to be mine, I’m not going to complain (ang oa [REDACTED_NAME] self)

[Long Read!] Is it wrong if I (20, F) am slowly falling out of love with my boyfriend (20, M)? For context, I'm an alumni suffering from a seizure and my recent episode somehow messed my memories up causing me to not remember much. During the day that I suffered from the said illness, he was there for me since he took a visit to check in on me after hearing me pass out on the other end of the line. He was the first one that I told about my memory problem so he helps me out with learning about my memories. For some reason, whenever I ask or open a topic about our relationship, he would divert the topic or often times not speak about it at all. For all I know, I'm with someone who I've been with for 5.5 years yet he mentioned that I wasn't in his life within those 5.5 years, leading me to some confusion. These unanswered thoughts lead me to feel out of place. It quite had gotten worse when I'm starting to feel not very much myself when I'm with him virtually or physically. I know that I should talk to him about this but is it wrong? Am I wrong? I don't want to hurt him because it seems like he loves me very much to the extent that he would willingly take care of me but the feeling of being distant has been growing every day and it's eating me up... It's like I'm starting to feel uncomfortable with my own lover

Upon the time it reaches its end of the semester, I sometimes wonder, "How come, have I ventured this journey from the beginning?". It already made me think to reconsider to take the same path or to think twice about it, knowing that I may be with the people I know, worth trusting? I simply do not know now, and I'm willingly to take advice from anyone and what I think it truly matters. Because all I ever wanted was to reassemble myself, reclaim my true self, and know that I truly am. Because right now, all I can ever think of, is the calm music of a Lo-fi that truly simplified my pacing and my mentality. By the time I read this message of mine in the future, maybe I'll come back and think how have I reminisce the good and the bad memories. We all have made progress of our own carved path, and yet maybe it was or not the one we've been looking for. I may or may not be with the right circle, but I know that I'll get through this, just like Ma'am [REDACTED_NAME] said, "Best Effort". I truly never thought she would have the same quote as I spoke in this freedom wall, but I'll remember it knowing that we are based on best effort than on luck. Till then, to strive forward. -Wolf

There may be a slim possibility that you're referring to me, given that this page barely gets any participants and the demographics are most likely to be composed of SHS students; making it easier to tell who's who if I already know how they talk and act IRL and in chat. That aside, I'm hoping you're the one who sent the anonymous message that I tagged. Whenever we interact during school activities, you always seemed calm and collected. But, I know for a fact na deep inside is may galit (or disappointment) ka pa rin sa akin. Because of my actions, your mental health worsened. However, wanting me to stay away because you have personal problems will just make everything worse; because in reality, I should've been there for you all throughout. [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] you deserve all the love you get. You have nothing to be blamed for, because I was the one at fault. Nadamay ka sa internal conflict [REDACTED_NAME] around that time, and I am terribly sorry for that. I had no choice but to set my feelings aside because the circumstances were dire, and I needed a way to force myself out of everything that was affecting my health. From days, to weeks, to months, I went to all my friends on and off, and consulted them regarding my situation. I was desperate to fix the mess I made because I made it seem like you were nothing to me. I didn't lose you because my love wasn't real, I lost you because I was stupid and helpless. It always pained my heart and soul whenever our eyes meet in and near the campus, because I had to pretend like I felt nothing at all; I had to act like nothing happened and I'm just a stranger to you. I did believe it was for the best, regardless of how you'd react. I wanted to have some sense of control so I decided to burn the bridges that connected me to everyone, including you; hoping that one day I'll be able to build healthier relationships, so that I could treat you better. Pero, [REDACTED_NAME] nakatiis. I realized that I really do love you. I still love you, and I always did. Believe it or not, it has always been you, N. I hope you now understand my side. Once again, I am very sorry this took way too long. Don't tell me to find a better girl, because it's you who I want, love, and adore. Please, I just want to be yours, for no other girl shall ever suffice. I am willing to wait until you're doing well so that we can try once again. This time, I'm no longer going to do anything stupid like leaving you in the dark. I will stay by your side for as long as you want, and nothing else will convince me to do so otherwise.

I saw her first and admired her from afar. She was so cute and gorgeous when I tapped her from behind with a flower. She was shy all day and I couldn't stop looking at her eyes. Everything around me went silent when she talked. Then the day went on taking each other time by our side. After a few weeks around February things went south. I wanted to pursue her properly and treat her. But she wanted me to be her boyfriend already. But the way she said that she's allow me to be her boyfriend was in a joking manner. And I couldn't get the hint that she was serious about it. So I joking said I don't believe her because she made it so easy suddenly, when I haven't pursued her properly. After that incident things went cold and soon didn't talk to each other. Because I didn't know how to please her to fix things. And I thought that she lost interest already. Few months passed around ber months we talked and had a closure. We admitted we were immature and there were no hard feelings. After us I tried to explore and meet others. Growth from a Heartbreak Share ko lang something personal. It all started because of this girl I met last semester. January , I met her. Let's call her [REDACTED_NAME]. She was a friend of mine and I was in love at her at first sight. She was a very special person because she is the catalyst for my personal growth to become a better person. She was also the first person to leave me after a negative interaction. A negative interaction I caused. I had a huge crush on her, but despite knowing she has a boyfriend, I still act flirty around her, which was wrong. I was already making her uncomfortable and I wasn't aware of it. The night I confessed to her on [REDACTED_NAME] and blocked me, I fell deep. That's when I realized a few things. I struggle with social cues, sometimes I don't acknowledge how people perceive my actions or how they feel about it, etc... Now, I'm much more cautious of what I will say or do.But it's something I still have to get used to. For all my life, I was also an inside kid, hence the reason of my struggle for social cues. I made a post about it too (). Looking back at this heartbreak, [REDACTED_NAME] cutting me out was the turning point of my life that forced me to grow. Since then, I've took losing weight seriously. I lost 12kgs since. Primarily because of severe depression and anxiety that I lost the ability to eat properly. But then I shifted it into a more positive light. I then started writing a diary about this. After almost 3 months, it has now over 7,000 words and counting. I've also been going out of my comfort zone, attending concerts even though I'm not usually someone who is a fan of them. I've been hanging out more with my high school friends in social gatherings. Because what I also realized is that friendships are also relationships that requires time and sacrifice. I also go to small events like the BTS Pop-Up store in MOA. Been exploring proper clothing too not just the usual t shirt with pants. It was a genuine heartbreak experience because like I said, she's the first person to leave my life after a negative interaction. Hindi [REDACTED_NAME], as there are times where I have moments of doubts and relapsing into old habits. But the progress I made kept me going. And I don't want to de-value the heartbreak. I'll honor the heartbreak by continuing what she gave me. Ayokong iwanan tong heartbreak [REDACTED_NAME]. Because like I said, [REDACTED_NAME] was the one who actually forced me to change. [REDACTED_NAME], if ever na mabasa mo to. I think you know who I am. Thank you, if it wasn't for you, maybe I'm still the same person you met. I no longer ask for forgiveness, I just want you to hear what I have to say. And if you ever want to read the diary, I'l be glad to give it you. Same title as this post. I also left a message there for you in the case you ever read the diary... Thank you. It's still a long way for me to go to become a well rounded and mature person.

What went wrong? We weaved a world of cozy cafe hangouts, our legs brushing under the table as we doodled on tissues and whispered our giggles. Late-night messages, sometimes fueled by laughter, kept us connected between our separate days. The excitement of stolen glances and touches filled me with a warmth I couldn't ignore. I fell for the care behind your 'I miss you's' and the glimpses into your world you shared through your pictures and life stories. I allowed myself to be vulnerable. But like the leaves scattering in the autumn breeze, the warmth began to fade. Replies turned infrequent, messages left on seen. The silence grew louder. Maybe someone else is getting the messages I used to wait for all day. Maybe...maybe I just got a little boring, or maybe I wasn’t too cool for you. Maybe this message will find its way to you, a whisper across the halls of our separate campuses. Even if it doesn't, a part of me enjoyed those times, a bittersweet reminder of the times that faded.

How do I get over this?? I have so many problems and so much shit on my mind.. I don't have an outlet right now due to reasons I can't quite share.. but I'm just want to talk to someone new.. but I'm so so so shy.. and I feel like I've developed social anxiety because every time nalang I go out I start shaking and overthinking that people are looking and judging me based on how I walk, look, dress.. etc. It's so hard to forget those years especially when I was so deeply in love but I was right to break up with you since I know you deserve better. I'm not trying to gain sympathy but I just want to let everything out so I can let go and move on.. I just wish that my mind can rest easy with the never ending what ifs. I can't look at things the same way with out remembering you.. How you smiled, how you laughed, how you make tampo.. I remember when we used to say that we'll always choose each other no matter what. But when I got time to think I got the courage to step up and tell you that I want a man to love you the way you deserved, I was secretly hoping it would still be me.. I was secretly praying you'd wait. But I can't hold it against you that you can't wait for someone uncertain to change. I know I am imperfect.. I am aware of the mistakes I've made.. all of them are unforgivable.. And I was honestly surprised we made it that far into our relationship. But I genuinely think that this is goodbye.. I don't ever think you'll look my way ever again.. I don't think that you ever thought about me coming back.. since there's someone else. And that's okay. From the bottom of my heart I wish you two the best.. I pray that he is the man that can love you the way you deserve.. I pray that he keeps you growing, loved, happy, grounded, and secure. And as for me, I don't know where I'll end up in the next few years.. But I just want to be happy and comfortable in my own skin. I was genuinely happy and forgot all about my insecurities when I was with you. And now that you aren't here to help keep those thoughts away, I need to learn how to get stronger on my own. To the readers I hope that whatever you are struggling with you find the strength and courage to keep going no matter how hopeless it may seem.. there will be many sleepless nights, racing thoughts, feelings of dread and misery.. but let's all try to be as positive as we can. I love every single one of you.

To that friend I fell in love with and became a stranger, I hope you are doing well. I just wanna tell you, I'm sorry. I wasn't respecting our friendship we had to the point it got damaged until it broke off. I was happy when we have long talks because I had feelings for you. But when I learned that you have a boyfriend, I was upset a bit. And I started accepting it mentally, but the way I act and speak, I was being flirty when we meet in person or chat online. I wanted you to be my friend instead, but in the long run, I was only showing you my true feelings which made you distance yourself from me. Everytime we chat and you were barely replying, I was so worried on the why and what, and everytime you and our other friend are walking together and I join, you leave and walk the other way. I didn't realize that it was me all along... I wish I could go back to the very first day we met and make the right decisions, but it's in the past, and the past can't be changed. You left me a scar in my heart, and that scar will forever teach me. Like [REDACTED_NAME] said "These scars we have, they make us who we are. We're not meant to go back and fix them." "Don't let it define you, live your life." That's just being human. We make mistakes, either we run from it or learn from it. Me? I'm still learning, but you made me realize my mistakes. You were the wake up call I needed to grow as a person. In our life, people come and go, for better or worse. You were also the first person in my life to leave in a difficult way. My past crushes, we ended with either friendship or just classmates, but you, you chose being a stranger. And that is all right. I respect your decision even though it is painful for me. I have to endure it, I have to carry that pain knowing that you are no longer with me. In the coming years, or in your lifetime, whenever you think about me, I know you'll only be reminded of the mistake I did, and I can't change that. We're only human, we think of those negatively to those who hurt us. But I just want you to know, people change either for better or worse. But you, you made me better. You made an impact on my life even though we known each other for only 3 months. For that, I thank you. I don't expect you to forgive me all of a sudden, I just want you to understand and hear my final thoughts about you. You may think that I’m a careless playboy with my actions, but none of those are true. I’m a good person, ask anyone. I have a good heart, but I still act childish sometimes. I’m working on myself recently, improving parts of my life especially working out, which I told you on my confession. I've been fighting my demons, it all started from the heartbreak. It's a tough journey, but it will be worth it. I hope you don’t remember me forever as the same guy you met. I’m slowly improving myself thanks to people around me, especially you. But it’s up to you. You’re out of my life. As hard as it is for me, I have to accept it. You may also think that I don’t care about what happened to me when you blocked me. But since after 7 days of the heartbreak, I finally cried. And then I decided on writing a diary all about you,how you impacted my life in positive way, and my journey to becoming a better version of me. The diary I made was not a reconciliation, but rather, a collection of my thoughts, feelings and how you helped me grow. I update it almost everyday when I think about you. Since [REDACTED_NAME] 18, it has now over 5,000 words. I also included some entries about songs there that I dedicated to you "FEARNOT" by LE SSERAFIM, "Spring Day" by BTS, and more.. But recently, I felt less and less caring. Eventually there will be a day where I completely moved on. But know that you impacted my life so much, you have a special place in my heart. I accidentally discovered which section and class you are in one subject because we have the same sections, different classes though. As much as I wanna see you, I know it's not right. I wanna show you the diary so bad, every thought I have, the things I learned. Everything I wanna tell you is in there. But, I don't know... maybe it will be bad and you may distance even more from me... It was my fault on why you switched sections when you found out I was your classmate. Trust me when I say I'm as uncomfortable and upset as you when you met the "fake" me. My heart was in the right place but my actions weren't. When we see each other again in [NCR] Tech or anywhere, I hope we have grown, especially me. On that day, I hope that... no, I know that I am a changed person. Thank you, E x-friend.
